body_txt: In folklore, incubi (the male version) and succubi (the female version) were demons who would sexually assault people in their sleep — the horrifying sensation of sleep paralysis, where you wake up and are unable to move, was known as "incubus" as late as Victorian times, a term borrowed from Latin where it referred to nightmares generally and literally meant "that which lies down on a sleeper". Originally, these demons were not vampiric, had no need to feed, and were neither seducers nor particularly attractive, being essentially sexual or spiritual predators who attacked their victims as they slept — by and large, art usually depicted them as hideous goblin-like beings. However, modern fiction writers don't want to use them like that , so the common traits of both succubi and incubi have become: Incubi and succubi are almost always treated as a species or type of demons , though rare stories instead make them a breed of vampires when they function similarly (with vampires feeding on blood for sustenance, incubi/succubi feeding on sex); either way, they're considered evil by default . In some legends, Satan himself changed shape to be both incubus and succubus. See, Old Scratch wants kids but can't produce human seed, so he becomes a succubus , receives some sperm from a guy, turns into an incubus , and passes it on to a woman. How this transmits satanic genes is a question not addressed as the theory was invented before Mendel's time , never mind Rosalind Franklin's. In some other legends, the first succubi and incubi were the children of Lilith . The offspring of these demons and a human are either demonic infiltrators of humanity or basically mortals with mysterious powers. The legendary Merlin is sometimes said to be the offspring of an incubus and a nun, prevented from becoming The Antichrist by a quick Christening. Incidentally, the above mythology went a long way to explaining away pregnant nuns in the Middle Ages without destroying their virtues. Vows of celibacy were taken very seriously. Several cases of actual rape in politically tricky circumstances also appeared to have been dealt with this way. Even more importantly, it explained why Merlin could use magic (which was seen as contrary to God's order), but still be one of the good guys. In the more recent viewpoint, with its much more lenient view of sexually liberated women (and sex in general), they tend to be Not Always Evil and their depictions feature a far greater number of subversions on the classic interpretation of succubi and incubi than straight examples. Most of them tend to be portrayed as Cute Monster Girls or Reluctant Monsters . They usually become a Succubus in Love , who ends up being redeemed once they find true love . Their Vampiric Draining powers are either non-existent, weak enough that they'd have to really overdo it to actually kill someone, or they are able to choose how much they drain; their newfound partner is often happy to give them whatever they need to live. But when Power Incontinence is in play, it's used for a relationship roadblock and angst as they Can't Have Sex, Ever without hurting the one they love. In more comedic works, writers might choose to have a younger and more inexperienced succubus run into a voracious, or at least experienced or skilled , partner for their first time, to give the amusing role reversal of the rookie predator being "eaten" by their experienced prey. For demons in general being attractive seducers, see Hot as Hell . For other depictions of demons in media, see the supertrope Our Demons Are Different . For other demons who tempt humans into specific forms of sin, see Sin Demons .
